rules.rule: withKernel(id, fn) is SYNC-ONLY. Async callbacks silently use the wrong kernel after the first await. For async code in workers, use getKernel(id) directly instead of withKernel().

Worker messages cross a serialization boundary. Kernel handles (branded types) cannot be sent across workers — only serializable data (numbers, strings, arrays, plain objects). Extract data before posting messages.
Use the taskQueue pattern from taskQueue.ts for managing async worker operations. Don't implement custom queuing logic.
